spec: offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-01-from-spool-drain-test-5-us216-still-broken-and-wake-on-power-verification.md (US-252 + US-253 source) + offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-01-from-spool-manual-sql-fix-not-in-migration-history.md (US-256 source) + offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-041-powermonitor-getstatus-non-reentrant-lock-deadlock.md (US-254) + offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-043 follow-up (US-255) + offices/pm/backlog/B-052-hdmi-dashboard-full-canvas-redesign.md (US-257) + Sprint 19/20 retroactive integration tests (US-260, US-261)
backlogItem: B-052 (HDMI dashboard, US-257) + TD-041 (US-254) + TD-043 cleanup follow-up (US-255) + Sprint 19/20 retroactive validation gates (US-256, US-260, US-261) + B-047 self-update e2e drill (US-258)

testBaseline.note: Post-Sprint-20 close baseline. main@2800c6d (V0.20.1). Verify exact count at sprint start.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Sprint 19 fixed US-216 trigger source; Sprint 20 wired PowerMonitor to power_log; BUT 5/5 drain tests have STILL hard-crashed at the LiPo discharge knee because the part of US-216 that monitors VCELL during BATTERY state and fires staged shutdown NEVER ENGAGES. Sprint 21's lead story (US-252) finally closes this 5-drain-test architectural failure. Plus paired wake-on-power verification (US-253) so the post-graceful-shutdown -> wall-return -> auto-boot loop works end-to-end. Plus 8 more high-priority stories around TD cleanup (TD-041, TD-043), Sprint 19/20 acceptance-gap retros (US-256, US-260, US-261), self-update e2e drill (US-258), drain-test harness expansion (US-259), and HDMI dashboard full-canvas redesign (US-257 / B-052).

EXECUTION ORDER (deps-aware): TIER 1 no deps -- US-252, US-253, US-254, US-255, US-256, US-257, US-258, US-261. TIER 2 deps -- US-259 (deps US-252; integration test consumes the fix), US-260 (deps US-242/US-244 from Sprint 20 already shipped). 8 T1 + 2 T2.

THEME: 'The ladder finally fires.' Sprint 16-20 built every layer of the power-down system; Sprint 21 gets the actual safety feature working. Post-Sprint-21 + drain test 6: graceful shutdown event in journal, no EXT4 orphan cleanup, clean boot record. Plus the dashboard CIO sees during a drain finally fills the HDMI canvas with useful information.

CONSTRAINTS (per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md): synthetic tests for runtime-only-verifiable stories MUST tightly mock at hardware/lifecycle-signal level. US-252's synthetic test must mock VCELL stair-step from 4.20V down past 3.42V over mocked time AND mock the BATTERY-state entry path AND assert all stage transitions write rows to power_log AND assert subprocess.run(systemctl, poweroff) called exactly once at TRIGGER. Mocking high-level abstractions (e.g., directly calling Orchestrator.fireWarning) does NOT meet the bar -- the bug class that survived Sprint 19/20 was exactly this kind of 'unit tests passed against high-level mocks but full pipeline never engaged.' Live drills (drain test 6, wake-on-power drill, drive 6 cold-start) remain POST-SPRINT ACTION ITEMS per CIO no-human-tasks rule.

GROUNDING SOURCES: 2026-05-01 Spool drain-test-5 note (architectural-gap diagnosis with timeline + 3 hypotheses) + 2026-05-01 Spool manual-SQL-fix note (TD-043 + US-249 schema-diff retro motivation) + Sprint 19/20 closure docs + Sprint 20 deploy verification log + 5 drain tests of empirical VCELL/SOC trace data + B-052 backlog item.

POST-SPRINT-21 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO rule): (1) CIO + Spool run drain test 6 with US-252+US-253 deployed; expect graceful shutdown event in journal + no EXT4 orphan cleanup + clean boot record. (2) CIO + Spool run wake-on-power drill standalone: sudo systemctl poweroff -> unplug -> wait 30s -> replug -> verify auto-boot within ~75s. (3) CIO + Spool run coupled drill: drain test -> graceful shutdown -> wall return -> auto-boot end-to-end. (4) CIO runs cold-start drive 6 with US-242 (idle-poll) + US-244 (BT-connect) + US-246 (defer-INSERT) deployed; verify drive_start fires within seconds + drive_summary metadata non-NULL + sync 200 OKs. (5) Marcus bumps deploy/RELEASE_VERSION at sprint close per feedback_pm_sprint_close_version_bump.md (V0.20.1 -> V0.21.0 expected; minor for new staged-shutdown firing path + new dashboard layout).

stories.title: US-216 staged shutdown actually fires -- wire VCELL-decline monitor to PowerMonitor stages
stories.intent: Across 5 drain tests over 3 weeks (Session 6 / Sprint 17 / Sprint 18 x2 / Sprint 20 post-deploy), the Pi has hard-crashed at the LiPo discharge knee (~3.36V). PowerMonitor correctly detects wall-out + writes transition rows to power_log (Sprint 20 US-243 ship), and US-234's VCELL thresholds are correctly configured (3.70V / 3.55V / 3.45V from Sprint 19), but the staged-shutdown stages NEVER ENGAGE: zero rows in power_log for WARNING / IMMINENT / TRIGGER stages, no systemctl poweroff event in journal, EXT4 orphan-cleanup smoking gun on every boot. Spool's 3 hypotheses (need Rex audit to distinguish): (A) staged-shutdown logic written but never subscribed to PowerMonitor's new callback chain; (B) VCELL polling happens but threshold-comparison logic is in a different module not subscribed to the polling loop; (C) thresholds are checked but the action ladder (systemctl poweroff at TRIGGER) calls into a still-dead path. After this story, drain test 6 should produce stage-transition rows in power_log + clean systemctl poweroff event + zero EXT4 orphan cleanup on next boot.

stories.title: Wake-on-power verification + deploy-pi.sh POWER_OFF_ON_HALT enforcement
stories.intent: Post-B-043-wiring production scenario: key-OFF removes wall power, US-216 fires graceful shutdown, Pi halts; key-ON returns wall power and Pi MUST auto-boot without operator intervention. Pi 5 bootloader controls this via POWER_OFF_ON_HALT EEPROM config (0=auto-wake on power return; 1=deep sleep, requires button or full power-cycle). Per Spool 2026-05-01 audit: chi-eclipse-01 has the right setting (POWER_OFF_ON_HALT not set -> defaults to 0). But this has never been tested end-to-end because all 5 drain tests crashed before reaching graceful poweroff state. Risk: an eeprom-config edit out-of-band could silently flip the setting. Fix: deploy-pi.sh enforces the correct setting on every deploy + adds a synthetic test that the deploy script's config-write step is correct. Wake-on-power drill itself is a post-sprint action item.

groundingRefs.value: POWER_OFF_ON_HALT=0 (or unset, default 0) means systemctl poweroff halts SoC + PMIC stays alive watching power state -- auto-wakes on wall-power return
groundingRefs.source: Spool drain-test-5 note Section 6 + rpi-eeprom-config docs

stories.title: TD-041 close: PowerMonitor.getStatus non-reentrant lock deadlock fix
stories.intent: Per TD-041 (filed Sprint 20 US-243 verification): PowerMonitor.getStatus uses a non-reentrant lock that can deadlock if called from inside a callback fired from within the locked section. Low likelihood today but becomes a hazard post-US-252 when staged-shutdown logic adds another consumer of getStatus. Fix: convert to threading.RLock OR refactor getStatus to read state without locking (snapshot read).

filesToTouch: src/pi/power/power_monitor.py (PowerMonitor -- replace threading.Lock with RLock OR refactor getStatus to read without locking; add docstring documenting choice)
tests/pi/power/test_power_monitor_db_write.py (UPDATE -- add test calling getStatus from within callback fired from locked section; asserts no deadlock)
offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-041-powermonitor-getstatus-non-reentrant-lock-deadlock.md (UPDATE -- mark resolved)

groundingRefs.value: Non-reentrant Lock blocks if same thread re-acquires; deadlocks orchestrator under specific callback ordering
Sprint 21 US-252 will add another getStatus consumer (staged-shutdown ladder) -- raises deadlock probability
Two valid fixes: RLock OR lockless snapshot read

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `Lock|RLock|getStatus|handleTransition` src/pi/power/power_monitor.py.
PowerMonitor.getStatus no longer deadlocks when called from a callback fired within handleTransition
Existing tests pass + new test demonstrates the deadlock is fixed
Docstring updated explaining locking approach + why
TD-041 marked resolved
Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors
invariants: Public API unchanged (handleTransition, getStatus, getCurrentPowerState)
Thread-safety preserved
Performance comparable
stopConditions: If deadlock is actually caused by ordering bug not lock kind -- STOP, document actual race
If lockless read can't guarantee multi-step coherence -- STOP, default to RLock

stories.title: HDMI dashboard full-canvas redesign (B-052)
stories.intent: Per CIO 2026-05-01: dashboard renders 480x320 (legacy Pi touchscreen footprint) on the Eclipse's HDMI display, occupying only a small fraction of the screen canvas. Goal: redesign layout to use full HDMI canvas (1920x1080 or 1280x720) with larger fonts + clearer state indicators visible at driving distance. Surfaces all Sprint 16+ states + post-US-252 shutdown-stage with appropriate color coding. Backwards compat: 480x320 layout still works (config-selectable) for dev/testing.

stories.title: Drive 6 cold-start lifecycle synthetic test (US-242 + US-244 + US-246 integration)
stories.intent: Sprint 20 shipped US-242 (idle-poll escalation), US-244 (non-blocking BT-connect), and US-246 (defer-INSERT). Each has unit coverage but no integration test exercises the full key-on -> idle-poll -> escalation -> drive_start -> sensor-arrival -> drive_summary INSERT -> drive_end -> sync lifecycle. Drive 6 (post-Sprint-21 action item) will exercise this. This synthetic test is the ground-truth gate.

filesToTouch: tests/pi/integration/test_drive_lifecycle_cold_start.py (NEW -- full lifecycle test; mocks BATTERY_V trace, ECU PID arrivals, IAT/BATTERY/BARO sensor arrivals, engine off; asserts drive_start fires at ~sample 6, drive_id minted, drive_summary INSERT after first IAT, drive_end at engine-off, sync POST 200 OK)

groundingRefs.value: Drive 6 is post-Sprint-21 action item; CIO+Spool will run; synthetic test must pass first
BATTERY_V signature: 12.7 (off) -> 11.4 (cranking dip) -> 14.4 (alternator on)
drive_summary INSERT deferred until first IAT/BATTERY/BARO reading per US-246

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `test_drive_summary_defer_insert|test_idle_poll_escalation|test_lifecycle` tests/.
test_drive_lifecycle_cold_start.py: orchestrates full mock lifecycle; asserts drive_start fires within ~6 BATTERY_V samples; drive_id minted; drive_summary INSERT after first IAT mock
Asserts drive_summary row has non-NULL ambient_temp_at_start_c, starting_battery_v, barometric_kpa_at_start
Asserts drive_end at mocked engine-off; sync POST emitted; sync_log status=ok
Test fails against pre-US-242/US-244/US-246 code -- documented in docstring
Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors
invariants: Test exercises real production code path
Test runs in <10s
Test must explicitly fail against pre-Sprint-20 code
stopConditions: If integration reveals bugs in production lifecycle -- STOP, file specific TD
If sync mock requires sophisticated HTTP server -- STOP, fall back to mocking HTTP client

stories.title: Sprint 18 retroactive integration tests for US-216 + US-228 (per feedback_runtime_validation_required)
stories.intent: Sprint 18's US-216 and US-228 both shipped passes:true but failed silently in production for weeks. Per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md (rule born from this lesson), synthetic tests should have been strong enough to surface the bugs. This story closes the retro loop: write tests that WOULD have caught those bugs, prove they fail against pre-Sprint-19 code, document them as exemplars. Doubles as refresher on what 'tightly mock at hardware-signal level' means.

filesToTouch: tests/pi/power/test_us216_retro_pre_sprint19_failure_mode.py (NEW -- mocks 4 drain trajectories from Drains 1-4; parameterized over both pre-fix-passes and post-fix-passes assertions; demonstrates the bug class is now caught)
tests/pi/obdii/test_us228_retro_pre_sprint19_failure_mode.py (NEW -- analogous for US-228; mocks Drives 3/4/5 trajectory; demonstrates Sprint 19 US-236 inverted-assertion path)
specs/methodology.md (UPDATE -- 'Retroactive integration tests' section citing US-216 + US-228 exemplars)

groundingRefs.value: feedback_runtime_validation_required.md: synthetic tests must fail against buggy code
groundingRefs.source: feedback memory + accumulated US-216 + US-228 + TD-043 incidents

groundingRefs.value: Drains 1-4: each crashed at SOC 57-63%, VCELL ~3.36-3.45V; battery_health_log empty across all 4
Drives 3/4/5: each had drive_summary metadata all-NULL despite Sprint 18 US-228 ship

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `test_orchestrator_vcell|test_drive_summary` tests/.
test_us216_retro_pre_sprint19_failure_mode.py: parameterized over 4 drain trajectories; asserts pre-Sprint-19 path produces zero stage transitions AND post-Sprint-19 path produces all 3 stages
test_us228_retro_pre_sprint19_failure_mode.py: parameterized over 3 drive trajectories; asserts pre-Sprint-19 path produces all-NULL metadata AND post-Sprint-19 path produces non-NULL
Both tests pass; both have docstrings explicitly documenting which production failure they would have surfaced
specs/methodology.md updated with 'Retroactive integration tests' section
Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors

Test docstrings document both directions: 'would have FAILED against pre-Sprint-19 code'
No production code changes -- test discipline + spec discipline only
